description: 17 queries over 7 RAG-reference papers (RAGAS, RAG_Evaluation_Survey_2025, Pipeline_Optimization, Fusion_Functions_Hybrid_Retrieval, Qwen3_Embedding, SPLADE_v3, Rethinking_Chunk_Size_Long_Document). expected_chunks with DB-verified identifying_quotes; binary relevance ground truth. Generated 2026-05-24.

queries.query: What MRR@10 does SPLADE-v3 achieve on the MS MARCO dev set?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: it gets more than 40 MRR @ 10 on the MS MARCO dev set

queries.query: What two distillation losses does SPLADE-v3 combine during training and what are their respective weights?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: two main distillation losses have proven to be effective
starting from SPLADE++ Self Distil4

queries.query: What Acc@3 does Qwen3-Embedding-8B achieve compared to GTE-large in the pipeline optimization study?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: 0.571 versus 0.412 for GTElarge
GTE-large (1024-dim) scores 0.412
achieving Top-3 accuracy of 0.571 versus 0.412 for GTE-large (1024 dimensions)

queries.query: By how many percentage points does adding a BGE cross-encoder reranker improve Acc@3 for the GTE-large pipeline?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: 9.4 percentage point gain
reranking bridges approximately half the performance gap

queries.query: How does reducing chunk size from 2000 to 512 characters affect GTE-large retrieval accuracy?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: reranking bridges approximately half the performance gap
semantically coherent boundaries are beneficial regardless of the specific segmentation algorithm employed

queries.query: What three quality dimensions does RAGAS evaluate in RAG systems?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: faithfulness (i.e. is the answer grounded in the retrieved context), answer relevance (i.e. does the answer address the question) and context relevance

queries.query: How does RAGAS estimate faithfulness without requiring human-annotated ground truth?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: we first use an LLM to extract a set of statements

queries.query: What is the definition of Recall@K as given in the RAG Evaluation Survey 2025?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: Recall @ K is the portion of relevant instances that have been retrieved over the total amount of relevant cases, considering only the top k results.

queries.query: How does the RAG Evaluation Survey 2025 distinguish between rank-based and non-rank-based retrieval metrics?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: whether an item is relevant or not, without taking into account the item
The rank-based metrics focuse on the sequential presentation of relevant items, assigning greater significance to the positioning of these items within the ranking list.

queries.query: Which embedding model architecture benefits more from larger chunk sizes — decoder-based or encoder-based — according to the Rethinking Chunk Size paper?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: Stella is a decoder-based model, whereas Snowflake is encoder-based

queries.query: What chunk size achieves the highest recall@1 in SQuAD and why does the dataset favor small chunks?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: 64-token chunks yield the highest recall @ 1 which is 64.1%

queries.query: What MTEB Multilingual score does Qwen3-Embedding-8B achieve and which proprietary model does it surpass?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: surpassing the previous state-of-the-art proprietary embedding model, Gemini-Embedding

queries.query: How many synthetic training pairs did the Qwen3 Embedding pipeline generate in total?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: approximately 150 million pairs of multi-task weak supervision training data

queries.query: What does the Fusion Functions paper find about how RRF generalizes to out-of-domain datasets compared to convex combination?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: convex combination outperforms RRF in in-domain and out-of-domain settings
a tuned RRF generalizes poorly to out-of-domain datasets

queries.query: Why is score normalization applied before convex combination fusion in hybrid retrieval?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: the range of f_S E M can be quite different from f_L E X
min-max scaling is the de facto method in the literature

queries.query: How do SPLADE-v3 and the Rethinking Chunk Size paper each evaluate retrieval quality — what datasets and evaluation approaches do they use?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: meta-analysis over more than 40 query sets
recall @ k trends for different chunk sizes and embedding models

queries.query: Both RAGAS and the RAG Evaluation Survey 2025 address evaluation without relying solely on human annotations — what approaches do they each describe?

expected_chunks.identifying_quote: reference-free evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
LLM-based evaluation methods
